originalTitle: ‘Front groups and fellow travelers’: state department attacks US left over Cuba
summary: New report denounces Havana and claims ‘campaign of subversion’ has infiltrated highest levels of US government
content: As part of the Trump administration’s continuing attack on left-leaning groups, the US state department has listed several activists and organizations as “front groups and fellow travelers” for Cuba, including Hasan Piker and Amazon Labor Union founder Chris Smalls. In a 100-page report released on Monday, the state department denounces Cuba as the “capital of 21st-century communism”, accusing the Cuban government of carrying out a “campaign of subversion” that has “infiltrated the highest reaches of the US government, recruited and cultivated generations of American activists [and] backed an unprecedented wave of leftwing terrorism on American soil”. The state department report alleges that the so-called campaign is “designed to turn America against itself” and singles out several left-leaning activists and organizations, including the Democratic Socialists of America, Isra Hirsi – the daughter of Minnesota Democratic representative Ilhan Omar – New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani, and journalist Amy Goodman, citing their criticism of US policy toward Cuba or participation in Cuba solidarity movements. Describing the DSA, of which Mamdani is a member, the report says the organization “serves as a particularly potent illustration of the ideological victory of Cuba’s effort to position itself as the spiritual capital of Third Worldist radicalism”. It accuses the DSA of maintaining a “fierce, almost religious commitment to the cause of the Cuban regime – not because the group is controlled by Cuban agents, per se, but because that commitment is now simply the de facto policy orthodoxy on the American far left”. Trump has doubled down on similar messaging in recent days, using the term communism at political rallies and presidential engagements alike. For more than 60 years, the US has maintained a sweeping economic embargo on Cuba, in addition to crippling sanctions imposed by numerous administrations. Despite US claims that its policies are designed to pressure the Cuban government, Havana and many international critics including human rights organizations say they have severely hurt the island’s economy. The report comes just days after the state department, led by Marco Rubio – the son of Cuban immigrants and one of the US’s most outspoken critics of Cuba’s communist government – imposed further sanctions on the country, targeting its tourism ministry and several state-owned companies. The sanctions come as part of the Trump administration’s “comprehensive push to end the Cuban regime’s malign activities, both in Cuba and across our hemisphere”. Rubio has long advocated for “maximum pressure” toward Havana, arguing that easing US sanctions only strengthens Cuba’s communist government. Throughout his political career including his time as a senator, Rubio has backed tighter economic restrictions and repeatedly accused the Cuban authorities of repressing political dissent and fueling violence across the region. Earlier this month, Cuba suffered its third nationwide blackout in six months amid an oil blockade imposed by Trump on the island of 9.6 million people, prompting many residents to say that “living like this is agony”.
chineseTitle: “前线团体和同路人”：国务院攻击美国留在古巴
chineseSummary: 新报告谴责哈瓦那并声称“颠覆运动”已渗透到美国政府最高层
chineseContent: 作为特朗普政府对左倾团体持续攻击的一部分，美国国务院将一些活动人士和组织列为古巴的“前线团体和同路人”，其中包括哈桑·皮克和亚马逊工会创始人克里斯·斯莫斯。在周一发布的一份长达100页的报告中，美国国务院谴责古巴是“21世纪共产主义之都”，指责古巴政府开展“颠覆运动”，“渗透到美国政府最高层，招募和培养了几代美国活动人士，并支持美国领土上前所未有的左翼恐怖主义浪潮”。美国国务院的报告称，这场所谓的运动“旨在让美国反对自己”，并点名了几位左倾活动家和组织，包括美国民主社会主义者、明尼苏达州民主党代表伊尔汗·奥马尔的女儿伊斯拉·希尔西、纽约市市长佐兰·马姆达尼和记者艾米·古德曼，理由是他们批评美国对古巴的政策或参与古巴团结运动。报告在描述马姆达尼所属的 DSA 时表示，该组织“特别有力地说明了古巴努力将自己定位为第三世界主义激进主义精神资本的意识形态胜利”。它指责DSA“对古巴政权的事业保持着强烈的、近乎宗教般的承诺——并不是因为该组织本身是由古巴特工控制的，而是因为这种承诺现在只是美国极左派事实上的正统政策”。最近几天，特朗普加倍强调类似的信息，在政治集会和总统活动中使用共产主义一词。 60多年来
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
